Spend & True CPL

Leads by Source

Conversion Rate (Clicks → SF Leads)

Lead Reconciliation (Meta vs Salesforce)

Gap = Meta's primary conversion event minus SF-confirmed paid registrations. The primary event follows the Jul 1, 2026 pixel migration: Souled-pixel Lead through Jun 30, Global-pixel CompleteRegistration from Jul 1 onward (Souled campaigns now run only on the Global pixel and optimize on CompleteRegistration; the Souled pixel is retired). The two events are never summed — during the dual-pixel overlap both fired on the same registration and Meta cannot cross-deduplicate them. Positive (orange) = Meta over-reports; negative (teal) = Meta under-reports or attribution lag on recent days. Gap % is hidden for periods with fewer than 5 primary events (too noisy to be meaningful).

Period Summary

Meta conversions are split by pixel and never summed: Lead = Souled pixel (primary event through Jun 30, 2026 — retired at the Jul 1 pixel migration); CompReg = Global pixel (primary event from Jul 1, the event campaigns now optimize on). During the dual-pixel overlap each registration fired both, on two pixels Meta cannot cross-deduplicate, so adding them double-counts. Gap = primary Meta event (Lead before Jul 1, CompReg after) minus Paid Leads. Paid Leads = registrations with a real Meta signal (Facebook click ID, or Referral Type Paid/Social); All Regs adds the UTM-stripped/organic ones for context. True CPL = Spend / Paid Leads.
